summary: Optic atrophy is not seen in polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy because it primarily affects choroidal neovascularization without injuring retinal ganglion cells or optic nerves. Unlike retinitis pigmentosa, methanol toxicity, or central retinal arterial occlusion, which involve direct damage to neural elements resulting in optic atrophy, PCV lacks this mechanism of neurodegeneration. $\boxed{D}$

Unlike retinitis pigmentosa, methanol toxicity, or central retinal arterial occlusion, which involve direct damage to neural elements resulting in optic atrophy, PCV lacks this mechanism of neurodegeneration. $\boxed{D}$. Answer choice: D
